metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem, pain metric, or value case has been articulated by Sasol across any field or call. No evidence of cost savings target, cycle-time reduction, headcount impact, error-rate improvement, or ROI discussion. The RFI response and 20 Aug 2025 demo touched on solution capabilities but did not surface a quantified pain or compelling event from the buyer. Without a quantified value case, there is no urgency driver and no basis for executive sponsorship. The highest-leverage move is to conduct a structured discovery call with the VP or Manager-level sponsor (Hendrik Steynberg or Deon Meyer) specifically to quantify the operational or financial pain that would justify a $200k procurement investment and create a compelling event.

champion.narrative: No champion identified. The most frequently engaged contact per Next Step is implied to be mid-level (Estee Larkins or a sourcing/commercial contact), but there is no evidence of advocacy, internal navigation, introduction to the Economic Buyer, or active pushing of the Zycus agenda within Sasol. The VP (Hendrik Steynberg) and Manager (Deon Meyer) are on the contact roster but have not been directly engaged on the business case or commercial terms, so their sponsorship is unconfirmed. The RFI stall and 173-day silence suggest either no champion exists or the champion has lost influence/priority. The move is to identify and cultivate a champion, likely Deon Meyer (Manager: the deal owner's manager / Project Portfolio Manager) given his role spans improvement initiatives and would naturally own a procurement transformation business case, by conducting a value-mapping session that equips him to advocate internally for restarting the RFI/RFP process with quantified ROI.

competition.narrative: Sasol is benchmarking against SAP Ariba and Taulia per prior intelligence (known competitor topics on record). SAP Ariba is the primary competitive threat given Sasol's SAP ERP footprint (large enterprise, likely SAP-centric). Taulia is a secondary working-capital/supplier-financing play. No recent call or field update confirms active competitive engagement, but the RFI/RFP cycle (Jul-Sept 2025) was inherently multi-vendor, and the stall may indicate Ariba or another incumbent is defending the status quo or Sasol is paralyzed by choice. The wedge: Zycus ML iAnalyze + iRisk deliver AI-driven supplier risk and spend intelligence that Ariba's transactional procurement suite does not natively provide at the same depth, and the Merlin agentic layer (if positioned) can automate negotiation and contract intelligence in ways Ariba's rules-based workflows cannot. The activation move is to position a head-to-head capability comparison (Zycus AI/ML vs Ariba's static analytics) in a design workshop or executive briefing, anchored to a quantified pain that Ariba does not solve.

decision_process.narrative: The call sequence itself is thin (no Avoma calls matched to this deal), but the Next Step history outlines a stalled process: RFI issued Jul 2025, demo delivered 20 Aug 2025, RFP promised for Sept 2025 but never issued, and by Mar 2026 the process 'has not gained much traction'. The Salesforce contact roster shows five roles (VP, Manager, Sourcing Lead, Commercial Specialist, Improvement Specialist), but there is no evidence of a formalized evaluation committee, no multi-round review, and no escalation to a senior decision-making forum. The EY roundtable re-engagement (Oct 2025) was an attempt to restart the process via a partner channel, but there is no evidence it succeeded. The approval chain is unmapped: no record of whether multiple approvals are required (field Multiple_approvals__c not populated), who those approvers are, or what triggers progression to contracting. The move is to map the formal decision process with Deon Meyer or Hendrik Steynberg, including committee structure, timeline, and approval gates.

decision_criteria.narrative: Sasol issued an RFI in July 2025, and Zycus responded with a demo on 20 Aug 2025 covering ML iAnalyze, iSave, iSupplier, and iRisk (per Products field). The RFI signals some formal evaluation criteria were in play, but those criteria were never shared with Zycus in structured form, and the promised RFP (which would have formalized decision criteria) was never issued. The Next Step (Mar 2026) notes the RFI 'has not gained much traction' despite an offer to run a design workshop to help define next steps. This suggests Sasol's internal decision criteria remain informal, incomplete, or contested, and Zycus has not gained visibility into the weighted priorities or must-have vs nice-to-have breakdown. The move is to secure a structured BRD or criteria-mapping session with Deon Meyer (Manager: the deal owner's manager / Project Portfolio Manager) to surface and align on the formal decision criteria.

items.name: Stage: Initial Interest → Qualified
items.summary: Opportunity was advanced to Qualified, but this looks procedural rather than earned — it lands on the same day multiple opportunity-health gaps (no decision maker, cold prospect, thin contact map) were flagged, so the stage may be ahead of the actual evidence.

items.name: Evaluation process clarity needed
items.summary: Flagged that we still don't have visibility into how Sasol will evaluate vendors or who influences that process — a basic gap for a deal now marked Qualified.

items.name: Org chart / key contacts missing
items.summary: Health check surfaced that no org chart exists and key contacts across the account haven't been mapped, meaning outreach is likely narrow and exposed to a single thread going dark.

items.name: APD review and approval pending
items.summary: An APD (account plan / discovery document) is awaiting review and sign-off — indicates internal groundwork is still in progress rather than complete.

items.name: Low prospect inbound communication
items.summary: Sasol's inbound engagement has been minimal, reinforcing that we're driving this relationship one-sided right now with little buyer-initiated activity.

items.name: Decision maker not identified
items.summary: No confirmed economic or technical decision maker on the account — a critical blocker given the deal just moved to Qualified.

items.name: Multi-threading gap flagged
items.summary: Engagement is currently single-threaded; the health check calls out the need to widen contacts to reduce risk of the deal stalling if the one active contact disengages.

items.name: Prospect gone cold — 14+ days no inbound
items.summary: No inbound communication from Sasol in over two weeks, the clearest signal that despite the stage bump, we are currently waiting on them with no active dialogue.

day_summary.overall: Sasol moved from Initial Interest to Qualified, but the flurry of health-check flags logged the same day tells the real story: this is a paper advance, not a momentum signal. The account has gone quiet (no inbound in 14+ days), there's no confirmed decision maker, and the org chart/multi-threading work hasn't been done — so the qualification is provisional until basic account intelligence catches up.

competitive_position.summary: Competing against SAP Ariba (primary) and Taulia (secondary) in a stalled RFI/RFP cycle that has gone dark for 173 days. Ariba is the entrenched threat given Sasol's likely SAP ERP footprint; Taulia targets working-capital optimization. Zycus has not positioned a differentiated wedge or secured a champion to navigate the competitive dynamics. The path forward is to restart engagement with a head-to-head AI/ML capability comparison (Zycus iAnalyze + iRisk vs Ariba's static analytics) anchored to a quantified pain, delivered via an executive briefing or design workshop.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: This deal reads as stalled and under-qualified: a real, buyer-articulated business problem exists, but no economic buyer, champion, or live decision process has been established in twelve months, and the entrenched SAP Ariba/Taulia stack has not been displaced from its evaluator role.

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded through a guarded, mid-level contact (Estee Larkins) with no confirmed access to power.
Close date has slipped three times over 14 months (from 2025-12-31 to 2027-02-26) with no buyer-driven trigger recorded for any push.

win_position.text: Sasol's RFI names a clear, quantified pain (sourcing-plan cycle time, manual tooling, knowledge retention) with defined success metrics (Business_Objectives__c, 2025-07-14).
No economic buyer or champion is engaged after 12 months; VP Hendrik Steynberg has appeared once (20 Aug 2025 demo) with no commercial follow-up since.
The buyer's own promised RFP (targeted for early-to-mid Sept 2025) never issued, and the sole active contact, Estee Larkins, has not replied to rep outreach since 18 Aug 2025.

deal_momentum.text: No verified buyer engagement since 18 Aug 2025 (email reply) / 20 Aug 2025 (demo); every subsequent touch on file (Sep 2025 through Jan 2026) is rep-initiated outreach with no logged reply.

A possible in-person touch is referenced in the current Next Step (Gartner conference, ~May 2026) but is unverified: no task, call, or dated follow-up exists to confirm it happened or what was discussed.

customer_commitment.text: Sasol has not committed to pricing discussions; Estee Larkins stated on 7 Aug 2025 that the team is "not focusing on pricing" and will develop its strategy only after reviewing all RFI submissions.
Sasol declined to extend the solutions demo beyond one hour, choosing to keep the session narrow and Sasol-question-driven (18 Aug 2025).

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS fields not populated, so no authoritative AI excitement read. Products scope includes ML iAnalyze and iRisk (AI/ML-driven supplier risk and spend intelligence), which are strong AI plays, but there is no evidence the AI narrative has been positioned or is resonating with Sasol. The RFI/demo cycle (Jul-Aug 2025) predates Zycus's recent Merlin agentic push, so the agentic negotiation and contract intelligence story has likely not been told. The move is to lead the re-engagement with an AI/Merlin-first narrative (agentic procurement intelligence) anchored to a quantified pain, positioning it as a step-change vs Ariba's static rules-based automation.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Sasol issued an RFI in Jul 2025 and requested a focused, one-hour demo addressing their specific questions (requirement on record). Zycus delivered the demo 20 Aug 2025, but the follow-on RFP promised for Sept 2025 was never issued, and by Mar 2026 the process 'has not gained much traction'. No structured BRD or decision criteria have been shared, so alignment to expectations is unmeasured. The design workshop Zycus offered (Mar 2026) to help define next steps was not accepted, signaling either lack of urgency, lack of internal champion, or misalignment on value. Until a quantified pain and formal criteria are surfaced, fit remains speculative.
